textPlain: Tangentially: There's interesting research out there indicating that cellular repair is guided and promoted by the local electrical fields from surrounding tissues.For example: "Treating Scars After Burns With Pulsed Electric Fields in the Rat Model" - https://academic.oup.com/jbcr/article-abstract/45/6/1553/772...I wonder if we (or at least, our descendants) will figure out limb regrowth before we figure out functional immortality.
This paper is from 2010. Can the OP discuss why this is relevant today.
So much stuff seems to work in rats and mice but not people.Perhaps we should genetically move humanity over time to be more rat like.
